root_query: antimicrobial peptide antibacterial MIC minimum inhibitory concentration broth microdilution experimental

databases.name: DBAASP — Database of Antimicrobial Activity and Structure of Peptides
databases.access_method: JSON peptide cards (`GET https://dbaasp.org/peptides?page=&size=` listing + `/peptides/{id}?format=json`); Alternative: Advanced Search CSV/JSON/FASTA exports (https://dbaasp.org/help).

extraction_strategy.notes: Walker ingests monomer peptides only (see manifest). MIC rows originate from peptide card `targetActivities[]` filtered to MIC measurements and bacterial pathogens.

databases.notes: Primary programmatic web source wired in specs/web_extraction_manifest.json (`db_dbaasp`). Extractor stores verbatim MIC numeric text (`measurement_value`) plus canonical measurement_unit labels (`mic_normalization`).

databases.name: DRAMP 4.0 — Data Repository of Antimicrobial Peptides
databases.access_method: Bulk Excel workbook placed at `data/raw/web/dramp_general_dataset.xlsx` (general_amps sheet mirrors DRAMP workbook columns DRAMP_ID, Sequence, Name, Source, Activity, Target_Organism, Pubmed_ID). Official download bundles are categorized by activity on the DRAMP site.

extraction_strategy.notes: Filters rows where Activity contains Antibacterial. Target_Organism free text is segmented on `(MIC …)` clauses shared with CAMPR extractor. Hepuristic parser → extraction_confidence=medium.

databases.notes: Overlaps extensively with curated literature databases (often including DBAASP); treat as supplementary rows and optionally dedupe on sequence + bacterial pathogen + MIC text when merging downstream.

databases.name: CAMPR4 — CAMP / CAMPR antimicrobial peptide compilation (HTML portal)
databases.access_method: `seqDb.php` listing pages scrape `seqDisp.php?id=CAMPSQ…` detail URLs; extractor pulls Title, FASTA sequence, Activity, Target, PubMed hyperlink.
databases.license: Institutional terms (ICMR-NIRRCH CAMPR portal); cite NAR CAMPR publication

extraction_strategy.notes: Only antibacterial cards whose Target field contains parseable `(MIC …)` snippets become rows — many curated sequences omit numeric MIC entirely and are intentionally skipped.

databases.notes: Operational label `db_campr4` matches coursework naming; URLs remain on ICMR CAMPR portal. Rate limiting defaults to ≥1 s/request to stay polite (`rate_limit_s` manifest knob).

scientific_papers.name: Ramata-Stunda et al. 2023 — Comparative Evaluation of AMPs for Skin and Soft Tissue Infections (Antibiotics; first author: Anna Ramata-Stunda)

extraction_strategy.notes: Table 1 (p.4): peptide names and sequences for all 11 AMPs. Table 2 (p.5): MIC values in µg/mL for E. coli, P. aeruginosa, K. pneumoniae, E. faecium, S. aureus, C. acnes. Supplementary Table S1: antibiotic control MICs (not extracted as peptide records). Assay: broth microdilution, MHB. 6 pathogens × 11 peptides = 66 MIC records.

scientific_papers.notes: Primary paper source. Open access CC BY 4.0. Covers 6 rationally designed AMPs (R1, R10–R14) and 5 reference AMPs (RP556, LZ1, AA139, PA13, Oligo10) against ESKAPE pathogens + C. acnes. All 6 pathogens are clinically relevant. Sequences and MICs in same paper — no cross-referencing needed. Broth microdilution method described in Materials section.

scientific_papers.name: Zhang et al. 2024 — Symmetrical GSG-centered AMPs vs Resistant MRSA/MSSA Strains (Microbiology Spectrum)
scientific_papers.license: Open-access publisher terms — confirm on article landing page.

extraction_strategy.notes: Project extractor uses PMC-style PDF pagination in specs/pdf_extraction_manifest.json (`pages_used: [4, 5]`). MIC values for W-peptides in **µM** (`measurement_unit` canonicalized to `uM`). Rows for MRSA 544 and MRSA 103; peptide sequences extracted from adjacent table lines.

scientific_papers.notes: Same corpus as repo PDF `spectrum.00265-24`; **not** the Frontiers PMID DOI incorrectly linked here earlier. Describes centrosymmetric W1–W5 peptides with clinical MRSA context; aligns with MIC values published in µM.

scientific_papers.name: Lee et al. 2023 — Trichoplusia ni Cecropin A vs Colistin-Resistant Escherichia coli (Pharmaceutics; first author: Hyeju Lee)

extraction_strategy.notes: MDPI pagination can differ between HTML and bundled PDF; specs/pdf_extraction_manifest.json sets pages_used: [9] for this PDF. Printed Table 1 reports MIC versus standard and colistin-resistant pathogens; values stored as µM (`measurement_unit` = `uM`). Record peptide rows for T. ni and H. cecropia cecropin A; exclude antibiotic controls.

scientific_papers.notes: Primary focus: insect and classical cecropin MICs versus standard and colistin-resistant pathogens. Parsed MIC in **µM** as reported. Assay defaults in manifest: broth microdilution, MHB-style conditions per article.

scientific_papers.name: Hu et al. — α-helical AMP benchmark with broad MIC panels (Frontiers Microbiology 2022-style)
scientific_papers.license: CC BY Frontiers publisher terms — verify on landing page.

extraction_strategy.notes: Manifest placeholder (extraction_enabled=false). TABLE 1 reports many peptides vs many bacteria; needs explicit column headers → pathogen mapping.

scientific_papers.notes: Suggested extension for high row count; not yet extracted in this repo.

scientific_papers.name: Melittin-derived AMP selectivity (MDPI Processes 2026-style)

extraction_strategy.notes: Manifest placeholder. MIC in µg/mL for analogues vs Gram-positive and Gram-negative controls in narrative/table form.

scientific_papers.notes: Suggested second PDF target for a dedicated small parser once the article PDF is pinned in data/raw/pdf/.

supplementary_materials.name: Ramata-Stunda et al. 2023 — Supplementary Table S1 (antibiotic controls)
supplementary_materials.access_method: supplementary PDF download from MDPI article page

extraction_strategy.notes: Antibiotic (non-peptide) MIC controls only. Do NOT extract as peptide records — out of dataset scope. Useful for cross-checking assay conditions and confirming pathogen susceptibility profiles.

supplementary_materials.notes: Included for completeness and cross-validation. Antibiotic MIC values against the same pathogen panel confirm assay validity. Not extracted as primary records.

reference_databases.name: APD6 — Antimicrobial Peptide Database (version 6)
reference_databases.access_method: web search and FASTA download (https://aps.unmc.edu/downloads); no bulk MIC export currently available
reference_databases.license: free for research and education (cite APD3/APD6)

extraction_strategy.notes: APD6 MIC data is being unified and validated (per APD FAQ as of Jan 2026) — not yet available as a bulk MIC export. Use APD6 only for sequence cross-validation (checking peptide_sequence against canonical APD ID) and peptide name standardization. Do not extract as primary MIC records.

reference_databases.notes: Reference database only — NOT a primary MIC source for this project. APD6 (Jan 2026) contains 6,309 peptides (3,379 natural + 2,290 synthetic + 373 predicted). MIC bulk export is pending unification per the APD FAQ. Useful for verifying peptide sequences and names retrieved from DBAASP or DRAMP against the canonical APD entry.
